$comment: Narrow, explicit, immutable registry of known pre-existing cross-release content-hash mismatches for a given (documentType, key, version) artifact identity. This file does NOT normalize or rewrite any hash — it records exactly which historical releases pinned a different hash than the canonical one, and why. CrossReleaseHashConsistencyTest fails for any mismatch NOT listed here exactly (same documentType/key/version/releaseVersion/observedContentHash). No wildcards. See artifacts/audits/cross-release-hash-consistency-audit.md and artifacts/audits/published-workout-immutability-remediation.md / peak-volume-policy-immutability-remediation.md for the remediation that changed the canonical direction of the WORKOUT_DEFINITION/PEAK_VOLUME_BAND_POLICY/PUBLISHED_TEMPLATE_BUNDLE entries below.

anomalies.reason: The combination's masterTemplate reference was mutated in place (v1 -> v2) without a version bump before 0.3.0-pilot was published, so 0.3.0-pilot pinned a mutated content hash for the same declared (TEMPLATE_COMBINATION, TEN_K__4D__INTERMEDIATE, 1) identity.

exceptions.reasonForCanonicalDirection: catalog/workouts/easy-standard.v1.json has been RESTORED to its exact original, earliest-published content (matching 1.0.0 and 0.1.0-pilot) as part of WORKOUT-IMMUT-001 remediation. The corrected/intended content (allowedDistanceAccountingModes, DISTANCE prescription mode) now lives on the genuinely new easy-standard.v2.json artifact, never under v1.

anomalies.reason: 0.2.0-pilot pinned WORKOUT_DEFINITION/EASY_STANDARD v1 with mutated content (the domain-content vocabulary reconciliation pass edited the v1 source file in place instead of creating v2). Restored and superseded by WORKOUT-IMMUT-001.
Same pre-existing in-place mutation as the 0.2.0-pilot anomaly above; 0.3.0-pilot republished the same mutated v1 content.

Same pre-existing in-place mutation as the 0.2.0-pilot anomaly above; 0.4.0-pilot republished the same mutated v1 content (0.4.0-pilot's own remediation task only concerned the TEMPLATE_COMBINATION defect, not this one).

exceptions.reasonForCanonicalDirection: catalog/workouts/fartlek.v1.json has been RESTORED to its exact original, earliest-published content — see easy-standard entry above for the full explanation; same pattern.

anomalies.reason: 0.2.0-pilot pinned WORKOUT_DEFINITION/FARTLEK v1 with mutated content, same pattern as EASY_STANDARD.
Same pre-existing in-place mutation as the 0.2.0-pilot anomaly above.
Same pre-existing in-place mutation as the 0.2.0-pilot anomaly above.

exceptions.reasonForCanonicalDirection: catalog/policies/peak-volume-bands.v1.json has been RESTORED to its exact original, earliest-published (1.0.0) content — INTERMEDIATE rows 26-40/30-45/34-50km. The corrected/confirmed rows (22-32/30-42/36-50km) now live on the genuinely new peak-volume-bands.v2.json artifact, never under v1.

anomalies.reason: 0.1.0-pilot pinned PEAK_VOLUME_BAND_POLICY/PEAK_VOLUME_BANDS_V1 v1 with mutated content (the INTERMEDIATE band-corroboration pass edited the v1 source file in place instead of creating v2). Restored and superseded by PEAK-POLICY-IMMUT-001.
Same pre-existing in-place mutation as the 0.1.0-pilot anomaly above.
Same pre-existing in-place mutation as the 0.1.0-pilot anomaly above.
Same pre-existing in-place mutation as the 0.1.0-pilot anomaly above.

exceptions.reasonForCanonicalDirection: PublishedTemplateBundle is a DERIVED artifact, not an independently-versioned source catalog identity (its BundleVersion is always inherited from its root TemplateCombinationDefinition's version). Its content depends on FindWorkout's key-only, highest-non-retired-version resolution, so its hash necessarily changes whenever ANY dependency's resolvable content changes, even though the bundle's own 'identity' (bundleKey+bundleVersion=1) never changed. Do not treat this as an independent source-artifact-level immutability violation — it is fully explained by the WORKOUT_DEFINITION and PEAK_VOLUME_BAND_POLICY entries above. All five historical releases pinned a different bundle-v1 hash from each other AND from today's freshly-rebuilt canonical value, because each release boundary saw a different combination of underlying dependency states (see dependency-version-cascade-audit.md for the full derivation chain).

anomalies.reason: Built before FindWorkout resolved a second (v2) workout version for any of the 4 affected keys — this bundle predates the vocabulary-reconciliation-era mutation entirely, but its own hash still differs from today's canonical because today's fresh rebuild resolves the newly-created, intentionally corrected workout v2 artifacts (FindWorkout picks the highest non-retired version by key).

Built after the peak-volume-policy v1 mutation but before the workout v1 mutations; also predates workout v2's existence, so it differs from today's canonical for the same reason as the 1.0.0 anomaly.
Built with the mutated (pre-restoration) workout v1 and peak-volume-policy v1 content.
Built with the mutated (pre-restoration) workout v1 and peak-volume-policy v1 content, plus the separately-registered TEMPLATE_COMBINATION v1 mutation (COMB-IMMUT-001).
Built with the mutated (pre-restoration) workout v1 and peak-volume-policy v1 content (0.4.0-pilot's own remediation only concerned the TEMPLATE_COMBINATION defect, not these).

exceptions.reasonForCanonicalDirection: Same derived-artifact reasoning as bundle v1 above — see that entry's reasonForCanonicalDirection.

anomalies.reason: 0.4.0-pilot is the only release that ever published combination v2's bundle; it was built with the mutated (pre-restoration) workout v1 and peak-volume-policy v1 content.
